# Structuring projects

```{r}
#| include: false

# settings, placed in a chunk that will not show in the .html file (because include=FALSE) 

# disables scientific notation so that small numbers appear as eg "0.00001" rather than "1e-05"
options(scipen = 999)  

```

## psych-DS

I have contributed a very small amount to the debate around [psych-DS](https://psych-ds.github.io), a data standard for psychological data. 'Standards' are specifications for both what *must* be done or not done, and what *may* be done to provide flexibility. By having a standard, and an (automated) method of checking compliance with it

I really like the concept, but also still have to be convinced about some of the choices made in psych-DS. For the moment, I recommend partial compliance with psych-DS: there are high level principles that make for a very well structured project that I try to employ everywhere; but there are also other aspects of psych-DS that are (currently) high-effort-low-reward.

``` text
github_repository_name/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .gitattributes
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .gitignore
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ code/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ analysis.html
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ analysis.qmd
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ processing_study_1.html
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ processing_study_2.html
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ data/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ processed/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_processed_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_processed_codebook.xlsx
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_processed_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_processed_codebook.xlsx
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ raw/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_raw_behavioraltask_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_raw_behavioraltask_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_raw_codebook.xlsx
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_raw_codebook.xlsx
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_raw_demographics_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_raw_demographics_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_raw_selfreports_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_raw_selfreports_data.csv
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ outputs/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ plots/
в”‚       в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ plot_1_self_reports.png
в”‚       в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ plot_2_behavioral_task.png
в”‚       в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fitted_models/
в”‚       в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fit_model_1.rds
в”‚       в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fit_model_2.rds
в”‚       в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ results/
в”‚       в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ cor_matrix_study_1.csv
в”‚       в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ cor_matrix_study_2.csv
в”‚       в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ LICENSE
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ methods/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ replication_instructions.docx
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_labjs.json
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_1_measures_and_procedure.docx
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ study_2/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ replication_instructions.docx
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_labjs.json
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ study_2_measures_and_procedure.docx
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ preregistration/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ preregistration.docx
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ readme.md
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ reports/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ preprint/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ preprint.docx
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ presentations/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ conference_presentation.pptx
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в””в”Ђв”Ђ tools/
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ project_creator.qmd
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ project_validator.qmd
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
```

Note that psych-DS specifies that the data dictionary/codebook must be a json file. I'm not yet convinced it is worth the effort of creating these for many users, as there are few R workflows that make use of them.

psych-DS principles

-   The earliest form of data must be preserved
-   Original data should never be modified
-   Different versions of the data should be kept separate
-   All transformations should be documented

## Sharing code and data, making preregistrations

### GitHub

https://ui-research.github.io/reproducibility-at-urban/git-overview.html

https://intro2r.com/github_r.html

## OSF

Linking github repo to OSF

## Licensing
